tluaReturnPackUnionAnyPosition.tlua(27,40): error TLUA1385: Function type notation must be parenthesized when used in a union type.
tluaReturnPackUnionAnyPosition.tlua(27,42): error TLUA7051: Parameter has a name but no type. Did you mean 'arg0: number'?
tluaReturnPackUnionAnyPosition.tlua(27,50): error TLUA7051: Parameter has a name but no type. Did you mean 'arg1: string'?
tluaReturnPackUnionAnyPosition.tlua(27,57): error TLUA1005: '=>' expected.


==== tluaReturnPackUnionAnyPosition.tlua (4 errors) ====
    // A pack constituent may appear in ANY position of a return-type union, not only
    // leading. Previously only a pack-LEADING union parsed; a non-pack first constituent
    // (`nil`, or a parenthesized single like `(number)`) sent the pack in a later
    // position into the ordinary type grammar, which misparsed it and swallowed the
    // FOLLOWING declaration in an error cascade.
    declare function leadingNil(): nil | (number, string)
    declare function leadingParen(): (number) | (number, string)
    declare function threeArms(): boolean | (number, nil) | (nil, string)
    
    // The next declaration must still be seen -- the old cascade consumed it entirely.
    declare function stillDeclared(x: number): number
    local ok: number = stillDeclared(1);
    
    // Each spelling round-trips: any-position parsing accepts a pack constituent in any
    // position, so the printer's display order does not matter to assignability.
    local sigNil: () => nil | (number, string) = leadingNil;
    local sigParen: () => (number) | (number, string) = leadingParen;
    
    // A bare function-type return is still parsed cleanly (no spurious "must be
    // parenthesized in a union" from the union-constituent path).
    declare function fnReturn(): () => number
    local fn: () => number = fnReturn();
    
    // The pack grammar is scoped to the OUTERMOST return union: it must not leak into a
    // nested union inside a function-type return, where a pack constituent is still a
    // parenthesization error (the same as `type T = (x: number | (number, string)) => T`).
    declare function scoped(): (x: number | (number, string)) => boolean
                                           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA1385: Function type notation must be parenthesized when used in a union type.
                                             ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA7051: Parameter has a name but no type. Did you mean 'arg0: number'?
                                                     ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA7051: Parameter has a name but no type. Did you mean 'arg1: string'?
                                                            ~
!!! error TLUA1005: '=>' expected.
